heroHeadline: Af-Pak Non-Escalation Holds Day 15; Pakistan-Iran Diplomacy Active; Afghan Deportation Crisis at 147,000+
heroSubtitle: The Afghanistan-Pakistan non-escalation framework reached its 15th consecutive day on April 24 without major cross-border military incidents — the longest sustained lull since Operation Ghazab-il-Haq began in February 2026. Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir spoke with Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi on April 24, continuing Pakistan's dual role as Af-Pak peace facilitator and US-Iran ceasefire mediator. Human Rights Watch confirmed ~147,000 Afghans have been forcibly deported from Pakistan in 2026 — accelerating since April 1 — including at least 9 journalists with valid documentation returned to Taliban rule. Urumqi Round 2 remains expected for mid-to-late May with no formal date set.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
